cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the text and find the relevant part. The article mentions that even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat. Dana says that's because the debris orbits at speeds up to 28,200 km per hour. The high speed explains why tiny paint dots have damaged space shuttle windshields so badly that they needed replacement. So the key point here is the high speed. The answer should be A. Let me check the other options. B says they are hard to track. The text doesn't mention tracking difficulty as the reason for the threat, but rather the speed causing damage. C suggests they can blow up into millions of pieces. The article doesn't say that small pieces explode; it's more about existing
